utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi teresa what's going on i heard that i heard that you're having a sore throat you're not feeling well

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah my throat has been hurting me for like four four days now and i think i had a fever last night because i was really sweaty but i did n't take my temperature because i was already in bed

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so four days ago you started feeling badly okay now were you having chills

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah last night i was chills and i had lot of sweating and it's really hard to swallow

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's really hard to swallow okay now do you have pain every time you swallow or is it just periodically

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: every time i swallow i'm even having trouble eating i can drink okay the like really cold water feels good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that's what i was gon na ask you okay so you're able to drink water and are you able to drink any other fluids have you been able to drink any you know i do n't know juices or milk shakes or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well besides my wine at night i really just drink water all day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay well i like to drink wine too what's your favorite type of wine

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: peanut grooves yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's a good one i like that too i am also a pino navar fan so there you go alright well let's now do you feel sick to your stomach at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i have a little bit of a stuffy nose not too bad it's really just my throat but i think my tonsils are swollen too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and your tonsils are swollen too now has anyone else sick in your household

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do have little kids that go to school so they've always got you know those little runny noses or cough but nobody is really complaining of anything

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright now have you had strep throat in the past

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: when i was a kid i had strep throat but i have n't had anything like that as an adult

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and what do you do for work

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i work as a cashier in a supermarket

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and did you get your covid vaccine

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep i did get my covid vaccine but it really made me feel sick so i'm hoping i do n't have to get another one later this year

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay did so you just got the two vaccines you did n't get the booster

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i did n't get the booster because i really have n't had time to feel that sick again it really knocked me down for like two days and with the little kids it's really hard

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well i saw that they did a rapid covid test when you came in here and that was negative so that's good so you do n't have covid which is which is good now let's talk a little bit about your hypertension and hypertension since i have you here did you ever buy that blood pressure cuff that i asked you to buy

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i did i blood the blood pressure cuff and my blood pressure is like all over the place sometimes it could be like one twenty for the top number sometimes it could be one forty for the top number i i do n't really remember the bottom number though

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that's okay are you taking the lisinopril i think we have you on twenty milligrams a day

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep i take it every morning with my multivitamin and my vitamin d

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and are you watching your salt intake

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i really like my chips with my wine

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is n't that the best we we could get along really well outside of here alright and then tell me a little bit about your diabetes now are you are you watching your blood sugars are you taking them at home

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sometimes i take that all that often again that could be all over the place sometimes i get if i take it first thing in the morning it'll be like eighty or ninety but at night sometimes it could be one forty

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and i are you still taking the metformin we have you on a thousand milligrams twice a day

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh uh yes i do take it i take it with my breakfast and with my dinner

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright great alright now are you are you a meds fan or a yankie's fan or god for a bit of filly's fan

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no do n't do n't like sports

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do n't like sports just the wine

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well let's go ahead i wan na just do a quick physical exam now i'm gon na be calling out some of my findings and i'm gon na let you know what that means when i'm done okay so looking here first at your vital signs your vital signs look pretty good you do have a low-grade fever of about a hundred . four right now but otherwise your blood pressure is pretty good it's about one thirty two over eighty and your heart rate is eighty four now that looks pretty good so i'm just gon na go ahead and examine you so on your facial exam i'm gon na just press on your face here does this hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no not no it does n't bother me

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay on facial examination the patient has no pain to palpation of the frontal or maxillary sinuses on nasal examination there is edema and erythema of the nasal turbinates bilaterally with associated clear discharge open up your mouth and say

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: ah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on throat examination there is bilateral erythema and edema of the peritonsillar space with exudates present bilaterally the uvula is midline on your neck exam i do appreciate some cervical lymphadenopathy on the right hand side on your lung exam your lungs you have some coarse rhonchi at the bases that clear with cough and on your heart exam your heart is a nice regular rate and rhythm i do n't appreciate any murmur or or rub so what does all of that mean teresa so all of that means is that yes you're showing signs of what we call an upper respiratory infection and i'm concerned that you might have some strep in the back of your throat based on the findings so let's just talk a little bit about my assessment and plan for you okay so for your first problem of your sore throat i'm gon na go ahead and have the medical assistant come in and swab you for a rapid strep test and if that's positive i wan na go ahead and place you on or prescribe amoxicillin five hundred milligrams three times a day for ten days and i'm gon na give you some lidocaine swish and swallow so that will help with some of the pain and you can take some ibuprofen as needed which will also help with the pain and some of that fever okay i do want you to go ahead and continue to to hydrate as much as possible what kind of questions do you have about that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no that sounds good i just wanted to be sure i was okay because of the little kids

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure now for your next problem of your hypertension i wan na go ahead and order a lipid panel on you and i think i do wan na increase i do wan na increase the lisinopril to forty milligrams once a day just to get your blood pressure under better control and we'll see how you do on the forty milligrams once a day for your third problem of your diabetes let's go ahead and order a hemoglobin a1c and just to make sure that we do n't have to make any adjustments to your metformin how does that sound

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any questions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope that's everything

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay bye good to see you i'll be in touch

detail_list.summary: Sore throat
The patient is a female with a history of hypertension and diabetes.
The patient reports a sore throat for four days and symptoms of fever and sweating.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing chills.
The patient reports chills, sweating, and dysphagia.
The doctor asks if the throat pain occurs with every swallow or periodically.
The patient reports pain with every swallow and difficulty eating, though she can drink cold water.
The doctor asks if the patient is able to drink other fluids.
The patient reports drinking wine at night.
The doctor asks if the patient has any gastrointestinal symptoms.
The patient denies gastrointestinal symptoms but reports nasal congestion and swollen tonsils.
The doctor asks if anyone else in the household is sick.
The patient reports her children have minor respiratory symptoms but no one is significantly ill.
The doctor asks about the patient's history of strep throat.
The patient reports a history of strep throat as a child but not as an adult.
The doctor asks about the patient's occupation.
The patient works as a cashier in a supermarket.
The doctor notes the rapid COVID-19 test performed in the office was negative.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient purchased a blood pressure cuff.
The patient reports fluctuating blood pressure readings.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking lisinopril 20 mg daily.
The patient confirms adherence to lisinopril, multivitamin, and vitamin D.
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring her salt intake.
Diabetes
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring her blood sugar at home.
The patient reports blood sugar fluctuations between 80-90 in the morning and 140 in the evening.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking metformin 1000 mg twice daily.
The patient confirms taking metformin with breakfast and dinner.
The doctor reports vital signs: temperature 100.4 F, blood pressure 132/80 mm Hg, and heart rate 84 BPM.
The doctor performs a physical exam of the face, nose, throat, neck, lungs, and heart.
The patient denies pain to palpation of the sinuses.
Physical exam findings: no sinus tenderness, nasal turbinate edema/erythema with clear discharge.
Physical exam findings: peritonsillar erythema/edema with exudates, midline uvula, cervical lymphadenopathy, coarse rhonchi clearing with cough, and regular heart rhythm.
The doctor orders a rapid strep test, prescribes amoxicillin 500 mg three times daily for 10 days if positive, lidocaine swish and swallow, and recommends ibuprofen for pain and fever.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions regarding the treatment plan.
The doctor orders a lipid panel, increases lisinopril to 40 mg once daily, and orders a hemoglobin A1c.
The doctor asks if the patient has any further questions.
